energy.label: Electricity
energy.description: Electrical supply available to keep the city running.
energy.mechanism_description: Electricity depends on fuel deliveries, generation capacity, and the workers who keep essential systems operating.

glossary.quick_explain: Electricity powers water treatment, heat, transport support, and emergency response.
glossary.why_it_matters: If electricity fails, multiple city services can fail together.

tutorial.player_hint: Protect electricity first when several essential services depend on it.
common_misreadings: A large draw on electricity is not automatically bad if reserves and imports are holding.

water.label: Water
water.description: Usable water available for homes, hospitals, and essential services.
water.mechanism_description: Water depends on electricity, repair capacity, and reliable movement through damaged systems.

glossary.quick_explain: Water service depends on power, pipes, and the crews who keep water moving.
glossary.why_it_matters: Losing water service quickly harms health and trust.

tutorial.player_hint: If power looks weak, watch water next.

food.label: Food
food.description: Stored and delivered food available to households and institutions.
food.mechanism_description: Food depends on transport throughput, labor, and enough water and power to keep distribution working.

glossary.quick_explain: Food represents both reserves and the ability to get rations to people.
glossary.why_it_matters: Food strain weakens health, labor capacity, and public confidence.

tutorial.player_hint: Food is often the next system to weaken after power and water.

glossary.quick_explain: Health shows how well people can endure the crisis.
glossary.why_it_matters: Lower health reduces workforce strength and raises visible social strain.

workforce.label: Workforce
workforce.description: Available labor capacity for production, unloading, repairs, and relief work.
workforce.mechanism_description: Worker health, fatigue, and unrest shape how much useful labor is available each turn.

glossary.quick_explain: Workforce capacity measures how much labor the city can actually use this turn.
glossary.why_it_matters: Low labor slows repairs, unloading, and service recovery.

treasury.label: Budget
treasury.description: Money available to keep emergency action going.

glossary.quick_explain: The budget limits how much emergency relief and policy action you can sustain.
glossary.why_it_matters: A weak budget shrinks future options.

trust.label: Public Trust
trust.description: How much residents believe leaders and institutions are acting fairly and effectively.

glossary.quick_explain: Public trust shows whether people still believe emergency decisions are fair and effective.
glossary.why_it_matters: Low trust makes every shortage more politically dangerous.

legitimacy.label: Legitimacy
legitimacy.description: The perceived right of the authorities to make hard emergency decisions.

glossary.quick_explain: Legitimacy is the public sense that the authorities have the right to govern.
glossary.why_it_matters: Low legitimacy makes sacrifices harder to sustain.

logistics.label: Transport Throughput
logistics.description: How much supply and repair activity the city can actually move through its constrained routes.
logistics.mechanism_description: Transport depends on crews, vehicles, facilities, and working institutions.

glossary.quick_explain: Transport throughput shows how much the city can move and unload in time to matter.
glossary.why_it_matters: Supplies do not help if they cannot reach people and services.

water_delivery.label: Water Service
water_delivery.description: Clean water treatment and delivery across the city.
water_delivery.failure_text: Water treatment and distribution may become unreliable.

glossary.quick_explain: Water service is the function people depend on, not just the water stock itself.
glossary.why_it_matters: Water service failures quickly harm health and trust.

heating_access.label: Heating Access
heating_access.description: Reliable heating for homes, hospitals, and other vital spaces.

glossary.quick_explain: Heating access tracks whether power and fuel are still keeping people warm.
glossary.why_it_matters: Cold exposure quickly worsens hardship and health risk.

food_distribution.label: Food Distribution
food_distribution.description: Movement of food from reserves and deliveries into households and institutions.

glossary.quick_explain: Food distribution matters because supplies must still reach people.
glossary.why_it_matters: Distribution weakness can create shortages even when stock exists.

transport_access.label: Transport Access
transport_access.description: Movement through routes, depots, and unloading networks.
transport_access.failure_text: Transport access may bottleneck relief and repair work.

glossary.quick_explain: Transport access is the city's ability to move relief, labor, and repair materials.
glossary.why_it_matters: Weak transport makes every other recovery step slower.

medical_care.label: Medical Care
medical_care.description: Capacity to protect vulnerable groups and keep hospitals running.

glossary.quick_explain: Medical care reflects whether hospitals can still function.
glossary.why_it_matters: Medical strain is highly visible and politically costly.

emergency_response.label: Emergency Response
emergency_response.description: Ability of institutions to respond to the crisis quickly and visibly.

glossary.quick_explain: Emergency response combines money, logistics, and authority.
glossary.why_it_matters: Slow response magnifies public fear and disorder.

public_order.label: Public Order
public_order.description: Visible social stability and manageable disruption.
public_order.failure_text: Disorder and incident pressure may rise.

glossary.quick_explain: Public order tracks whether daily life remains stable enough for recovery work.
glossary.why_it_matters: Visible disorder makes every shortage harder to manage.

water.label: Water
water.description: Emergency water supply for direct relief and service support.

electricity.label: Power
electricity.description: Emergency imported electricity or equivalent direct power support.

fuel.label: Fuel
fuel.description: Fuel used to sustain electricity generation and heating.

materials.label: Repair Materials
materials.description: Materials used to keep infrastructure functioning.

labor_hours.label: Labor Hours
labor_hours.description: Available labor effort for unloading, repair, and service support.

coal.label: Coal
coal.description: Solid fuel central to the airlift and heating emergency.

rail_capacity.label: Rail Capacity
rail_capacity.description: Throughput available on usable rail links.

foreign_aid.label: Foreign Aid
foreign_aid.description: Outside support that helps sustain the city.

energy_instability.label: Power may fail soon
energy_instability.summary: Electricity is at risk of falling below what essential services need.
energy_instability.if_ignored: If electricity falls further, heating, water, and emergency response may weaken together.
energy_instability.mitigation_hint: Import power or fuel, or reduce demand.

water_shortage.label: Water delivery may break down
water_shortage.summary: Water service is at risk of becoming unreliable.
water_shortage.if_ignored: If water weakens, health and food security may worsen next.
water_shortage.mitigation_hint: Protect electricity, add water, and reduce leakage.

food_collapse.label: Food stores are running low
food_collapse.summary: Food reserves and distribution are under strain.
food_collapse.if_ignored: If food keeps falling, health and workforce strength may decline.
food_collapse.mitigation_hint: Import food and protect water and transport.

budget_erosion.label: The budget is under pressure
budget_erosion.summary: Emergency spending is eroding fiscal room.
budget_erosion.if_ignored: If the budget keeps shrinking, future response options will narrow.
budget_erosion.mitigation_hint: Spend carefully and restore core systems first.

unrest_spike.label: Workforce strain is rising
unrest_spike.summary: Social and labor pressure is getting harder to manage.
unrest_spike.if_ignored: If strain rises further, service and income problems may spread.
unrest_spike.mitigation_hint: Protect visible services and avoid preventable hardship.

institutional_breakdown.label: Institutions may jam up
institutional_breakdown.summary: Transport, distribution, and administration are under stress.
institutional_breakdown.if_ignored: If institutions stall, even available supplies may not reach people.
institutional_breakdown.mitigation_hint: Support throughput, repairs, and frontline operations.

political_stalemate.label: Support for strong action is weakening
political_stalemate.summary: Coalition support is becoming harder to hold together.
political_stalemate.if_ignored: If support falls further, stronger policies may become unavailable.
political_stalemate.mitigation_hint: Choose visible relief that key actors can still defend.

public_trust_collapse.label: Public trust is slipping
public_trust_collapse.summary: Residents are losing confidence in the response.
public_trust_collapse.if_ignored: If trust falls, backlash and instability may spread faster.
public_trust_collapse.mitigation_hint: Protect the services people see and feel most directly.

households.label: Urban Households
households.description: Families facing cold homes, thinner supplies, and service disruption.

industrial_workers.label: Industrial Workers
industrial_workers.description: Workers carrying long shifts and ration fatigue.

hospital_patients.label: Hospital Patients
hospital_patients.description: Medically vulnerable residents who rely on stable power, heat, and water.
